title: Checkmate

days.evidence: Guren's dad: 'It's the weekend, Dad. Do you really have to go to work?' — with ep 19 an on-screen Sunday (day 37) and ep 20 a school day, the next weekend day is Saturday, day 43. Days 39–42 pass off-screen (gap days). One day on screen: morning at Guren's, park chess, the trap battle, the fortress reveal.

start.situation: Vilius's assembled army stands ready — 'No force on the planet could withstand such an army' — and he isn't stopping at Quarton. On Earth it's a weekend morning: Guren's dad heads to Benham Tower ('last stages of construction'), and Gen has picked his next target — Chooki's confidence.

beni.where: Benham City — off-screen this episode; exact whereabouts unshown
beni.doing: No on-screen moves; the army she flagged in ep 20 is about to move
beni.evidence: ep21 has no Beni scenes (one stray one-word speaker tag aside); analysis--arc-2 <!--ep:21--> covers Gen's Chooki gambit and Vilius's army

start.firstContact: *Weekend crowd in the plaza. She drifts through it at half the crowd's speed, licking an ice pop, reading storefronts like headlines — a girl with an entire empty Saturday and no intention of explaining it to anyone. Her eyes pass over you the way they pass over pigeons.*

goals.text: Break the 'weak link' that isn't one yet: beat Chooki at his own perfection — the Grand Master queen-sacrifice — because 'weaken one of the Knights, and all of the Knights are weakened.' Then run the same exchange on the battlefield.
goals.evidence: ep21: Gen: 'Let's see how he handles not being the best at something. It might shake his confidence a little.'

goals.text: Use the battle as a diversion and take the real army — on a Dragon-powered levitation platform — above the clouds, to the Guardians who banished him: 'Now, at last, I have the power to return the favor!'
goals.evidence: ep21: Vilius: 'They were just a diversion. No! The real battle will take place up there! Above the clouds!'

goals.text: Survive the queen-exchange trap and the fortress guns — Chooki turns his chess loss into the day's save: 'unlike chess, we can't afford to sacrifice a single one of us.'
goals.evidence: ep21: Lydendor: 'No exchanging queens this time!' / 'If we hadn't kept our distance, we'd all have been Tenkai toast.'

goals.text: Weather the council's hardening line — 'Eurus's actions risk throwing that balance into total disarray… he must be stopped before it is too late' — while keeping his own game alive.
goals.evidence: ep21: council: 'Be careful, Eurus. Moves can have hidden consequences.'

actors.doing: The chess op on Chooki, then the mirrored battlefield gambit with Granox as the cannon; ends the day satisfied: 'The board is set. The pieces are all in place. Now, let the game begin.'
actors.why: Psychological attrition serves the Key plan — and Vilius ordered the Knights crushed.
actors.evidence: ep21: Vilius: 'I need you to destroy the Tenkai Knights.' / Gen: 'Consider them crushed.'

actors.doing: Springs the real move: the army rises on a levitation platform 'powered with enough energy from the Tenkai Dragon to lift an army into the sky', and he confronts the Guardians in person over his ancient banishment.
actors.why: 'Defenders of Quarton! But who defends you?'
actors.evidence: ep21: the Guardians: 'You are forbidden here, warlord. … By attacking us, you will only bring destruction on yourself.'

actors.doing: A weekend morning that turns into the queen-trap battle; Chooki studies his chess loss mid-day and cashes the lesson in twice — the draw against Dromus, and the call that keeps everyone out of the fortress's range.
actors.why: 'Every chess piece has its own strengths. You can't win the game without all of them.'
actors.evidence: ep21: Mr. White's chess-pieces talk; Lydendor's paranoia vindicated.

actors.doing: Granox robofuses into Centaur Mode as the long-range cannon of Dromus's trap — then abandons the script for 'juicier targets at point-blank range.'
actors.why: Dragon-enhanced and finally trusted with a role; discipline optional.

actors.doing: Close ranks against Eurus in council — then find Vilius himself at their door with an army above the clouds.
actors.evidence: ep21: 'I do not know what Eurus is up to, but he must be stopped before it is too late.'

quarton.situation: The Corrupted's main fortress FLIES now — lifted on a Dragon-energy levitation platform with the full army aboard. The ground battle was a screen; the Knights fought it to a draw and only Lydendor's caution kept them out of the fortress guns' kill zone. The war's ceiling moved to the sky, and its next target is the Guardians themselves.
quarton.evidence: ep21: 'That's gotta be the Corrupted's main fortress. … To get us in range of the fortress's defenses.'

artifacts.event: extracted energy powers the levitation platform that lifts the Corrupted fortress and army into the sky
artifacts.evidence: ep21: Vilius: 'A levitation platform. Powered with enough energy from the Tenkai Dragon to lift an army into the sky!'

artifacts.event: assaulted — Vilius, once banished by the Guardians, brings his sky army to their sanctum to 'return the favor'; the confrontation's outcome is not shown this episode
artifacts.evidence: ep21: Vilius: 'I seem to recall you banishing me once long ago. Now, at last, I have the power to return the favor!'

outcome: The fortress is airborne, the army is aboard, and Vilius stands at the Guardians' door mid-threat as the episode ends. The Knights survived the queen-trap and learned the day's chess lesson twice over. Gen considers the board set. Benham Tower — where the Key sleeps — is in its last stages of construction, and Guren's dad works weekends now.
recall: Told him. I flagged that army days ago, and this weekend it moved — the whole Corrupted fortress went AIRBORNE, riding Dragon power, army and all. You don't miss a thing like that; the sky changed shape. Gen spent his Saturday playing chess games, board ones and battlefield ones, and calling it strategy. Fine. His board, his pieces. I keep my own ledger of who saw the avalanche coming first.

assumed: Saturday day 43 (weekend stated; minimal consistent weekend after ep 19's Sunday anchor)
unknown: Beni's actual activities this day and during the day 39–42 gap (off-screen)
how and when she learns of the fortress lift and the Guardian assault
